purpose: Declarations, counties, dates, and the program-declared flags (pa/ia/ih/hm).

fields.name: declarationDate
fields.title: Declaration Date
fields.description: Date the disaster was declared

fields.name: declarationRequestNumber
fields.title: Declaration Request Number
fields.description: Number assigned to the declaration request

fields.name: declarationTitle
fields.title: Declaration Title
fields.description: Title for the disaster

fields.name: declarationType
fields.title: Declaration Type
fields.description: Two character code that defines if this is a major disaster, fire management, or emergency declaration. For more information on the disaster process, please visit https://www.fema.gov/disasters/how-declared

fields.name: designatedArea
fields.title: Designated Area
fields.description: The name or phrase describing the geographic area that was included in the declaration

fields.name: designatedIncidentTypes
fields.title: Declared Incident types
fields.description: A comma-separated list of incident types designated for the disaster. The primary incident type is described in the incidentType field. Codes are: 0: Not applicable; 1: Explosion; 2: Straight-Line Winds; 3: Tidal Wave; 4: Tropical Storm; 5: Winter Storm; 8: Tropical Depression; A: Tsunami; B: Biological; C: Coastal Storm; D: Drought; E: Earthquake; F: Flood; G: Freezing; H: Hurricane; I: Terrorist; J: Typhoon; K: Dam/Levee Break; L: Chemical; M: Mud/Landslide; N: Nuclear; O: Severe Ice Storm; P: Fishing Losses; Q: Crop Losses; R: Fire; S: Snowstorm; T: Tornado; U: Civil Unrest; V: Volcanic Eruption; W: Severe Storm; X: Toxic Substances; Y: Human Cause; Z: Other

fields.name: disasterCloseoutDate
fields.title: Disaster Closeout Date
fields.description: Date all financial transactions for all programs are completed

fields.name: disasterNumber
fields.title: Disaster Number
fields.description: Sequentially assigned number used to designate an event or incident declared as a disaster.  For more information on the disaster process, please visit https://www.fema.gov/disasters/how-declared

fields.name: femaDeclarationString
fields.title: FEMA Declaration String
fields.description: Agency standard method for uniquely identifying Stafford Act declarations - Concatenation of declaration type, disaster number and state code. Ex: DR-4393-NC

fields.name: fipsCountyCode
fields.title: FIPS County Code
fields.description: FIPS three-digit numeric code used to identify counties and county equivalents in the United States, the District of Columbia, US territories, outlying areas of the US and freely associated states. Please note that Indian Reservations are not counties and thus will not have a FIPS county code, please utilize the placeCode field instead. If the designation is made for the entire state, this value will be 000 as multiple (all) counties cannot be entered.

fields.name: fipsStateCode
fields.title: FIPS State Code
fields.description: FIPS two-digit numeric code used to identify the United States, the District of Columbia, US territories, outlying areas of the US and freely associated states

fields.name: fyDeclared
fields.title: FY Declared
fields.description: Fiscal year in which the disaster was declared

fields.name: hash
fields.title: Hash
fields.description: MD5 Hash of the fields and values of the record

fields.name: hmProgramDeclared
fields.title: HM Program Declared
fields.description: Denotes whether the Hazard Mitigation program was declared for this disaster. For more information on the program, please visit https://www.fema.gov/grants/mitigation/hazard-mitigation

fields.name: iaProgramDeclared
fields.title: IA Program Declared
fields.description: Denotes whether the Individual Assistance program was declared for this disaster. For more information on the program, please visit https://www.fema.gov/assistance/individual/program. To determine which FEMA events have been authorized to receive Individual Assistance, use both ihProgramDeclared and iaProgramDeclared. For more information see https://www.fema.gov/about/openfema/faq

fields.name: id
fields.title: ID
fields.description: Unique ID assigned to the record

fields.name: ihProgramDeclared
fields.title: IH Program Declared
fields.description: Denotes whether the Individuals and Households program was declared for this disaster. For more information on the program, please visit https://www.fema.gov/assistance/individual/program. To determine which FEMA events have been authorized to receive Individual Assistance, use both ihProgramDeclared and iaProgramDeclared. For more information see https://www.fema.gov/about/openfema/faq

fields.name: incidentBeginDate
fields.title: Incident Begin Date
fields.description: Date the incident itself began

fields.name: incidentEndDate
fields.title: Incident End Date
fields.description: Date the incident itself ended

fields.name: incidentId
fields.title: Incident Identifier
fields.description: Unique identifier for an incident. Incidents are events that may or may not become declared disasters.

fields.name: incidentType
fields.title: Incident Type
fields.description: The primary or official type of incident such as fire or flood. Secondary incident types may have been designated. See the designatedIncidentTypes field. For more information on incident types, please visit https://www.fema.gov/disasters/how-declared.

fields.name: lastIAFilingDate
fields.title: Last IA Filing Date
fields.description: Last date when IA requests can be filed. Data available after 1998 only. The date only applies if IA has been approved for the disaster.

fields.name: lastRefresh
fields.title: Last Refresh
fields.description: Date the record was last updated in the API data store

fields.name: paProgramDeclared
fields.title: PA Program Declared
fields.description: Denotes whether the Public Assistance program was declared for this disaster. For more information on the program, please visit https://www.fema.gov/assistance/public/program-overview

fields.name: placeCode
fields.title: Place Code
fields.description: A unique code system FEMA uses internally to recognize locations that takes the numbers '99' + the 3-digit county FIPS code. There are some declared locations that dont have recognized FIPS county codes in which case we assigned a unique identifier

fields.name: region
fields.title: Region
fields.description: Number (1-10) used to represent the FEMA region where the disaster occurred.

fields.name: state
fields.title: State
fields.description: The name or phrase describing the U.S. state, district, or territory

fields.name: tribalRequest
fields.title: Tribal Request
fields.description: Denotes that a declaration request was submitted directly to the President, independently of a state, by a Tribal Nation.
